timeline.event: Harry Nyquist (1924) and Ralph Hartley (1928) lay the quantitative groundwork for information theory at Bell Labs.

Claude Shannon publishes 'A Mathematical Theory of Communication', introducing entropy, the bit, and the source coding theorem (entropy H as the lossless compression lower bound); Shannon and Fano develop Shannon-Fano coding; the paper also sketches the rate for a source relative to a fidelity criterion, seeding rate-distortion theory.
Shannon estimates the entropy of printed English at ~1-1.5 bits per character via human next-letter prediction experiments.
David Huffman publishes the optimal prefix-code construction ('A Method for the Construction of Minimum-Redundancy Codes', Proceedings of the IRE).
Shannon's 'Coding Theorems for a Discrete Source with a Fidelity Criterion' (IRE Convention Record) formalizes and names the rate-distortion function R(D) for lossy compression.

Ray Solomonoff introduces algorithmic probability, shortest-program description, and universal inductive inference, reducing optimal prediction to data compression and grounding the later compression-equals-prediction argument.
Andrey Kolmogorov independently defines the complexity of an individual string (Kolmogorov complexity), the shortest-program length of a string.
Gregory Chaitin independently develops algorithmic complexity theory and later defines the uncomputable halting probability Omega.
Nasir Ahmed, T. Natarajan, and K. R. Rao publish the Discrete Cosine Transform (DCT) in IEEE Transactions on Computers, a fast signal-independent approximation to the KLT.
Jorma Rissanen and Richard Pasco independently publish practical finite-precision arithmetic coding, decoupling the probabilistic model from the bit-coder.

Abraham Lempel and Jacob Ziv publish LZ77 (sliding-window dictionary coding) in IEEE Trans. Information Theory; Karlheinz Brandenburg begins research on perceptual audio coding at the University of Erlangen-Nuremberg.

Lempel and Ziv publish LZ78 (explicit-dictionary variant); Jorma Rissanen publishes 'Modeling by Shortest Data Description', coining the Minimum Description Length (MDL) principle and the two-part code.
Rissanen and Langdon publish 'Arithmetic Coding' (IBM J. Res. Dev.); G. Nigel N. Martin presents range coding at Southampton.
Boris Ryabko describes the Move-To-Front (book stack) transform (later independently presented by Bentley, Sleator, Tarjan and Wei in 1986).
Krichevsky and Trofimov give the add-1/2 estimator with ~(1/2)log2(n)-per-parameter universal redundancy.

Terry Welch (Sperry) publishes LZW ('A Technique for High-Performance Data Compression', IEEE Computer, June); John Cleary and Ian Witten introduce PPM (Prediction by Partial Matching), with variants PPMA and PPMB.
LZW patent US 4,558,302 issued (December).
Princen and Bradley publish analysis/synthesis filter banks based on time-domain aliasing cancellation (TDAC), the precursor to the MDCT.

Princen, Johnson, and Bradley introduce the full MDCT at ICASSP (the lapped transform underlying modern lossy audio codecs); Witten, Neal, and Cleary publish the widely-used practical arithmetic coding implementation (Communications of the ACM); CompuServe's GIF format adopts LZW compression.
ITU-T H.261 establishes the macroblock + motion-compensation + DCT template for video coding.
IBM files/obtains the QM-coder patent US 4,905,297 (granted Feb 1990), part of the arithmetic-coding patent thicket.
JJ Johnston (Bell Labs) defines perceptual entropy and builds PXFM; Brandenburg's OCF and these ideas merge into ASPEC.
Alistair Moffat improves PPM with variants PPMC and PPMD; Manber and Myers introduce suffix arrays (SODA), the data structure that makes practical BWT construction feasible.
Phil Katz designs DEFLATE for PKZIP 2 (later RFC 1951, 1996).

MP3 (MPEG-1 Audio Layer III) standardized at Fraunhofer IIS: committee draft 1991, finalized 1992, published 1993 as ISO/IEC 11172-3, bringing psychoacoustic masking and the MDCT to mass audio compression.

JPEG image standard published as ITU-T T.81 / ISO 10918-1 (DCT + perceptually-tuned quantization + Huffman; Wallace's explanatory papers 1991-1992); it specifies an arithmetic-coding mode (~10% better) but the free IJG implementation ships only Huffman; MPEG-1 video standardized, introducing the I/P/B frame structure.

Burrows and Wheeler publish the Burrows-Wheeler Transform in DEC SRC Research Report 124, 'A Block-Sorting Lossless Data Compression Algorithm'; Unisys announces GIF/LZW royalty enforcement (December).
MPEG-2 / H.262 finalized; becomes the codec of DVD and digital television.
The '.mp3' file extension chosen via an internal Fraunhofer IIS poll (July).

Julian Seward releases bzip2 (18 July), the canonical RLE+BWT+MTF+RLE+Huffman pipeline (Huffman chosen to avoid arithmetic-coding patents); PNG released as a lossless, patent-free alternative to GIF using prediction filters plus DEFLATE and adding an alpha channel.
AAC standardized as MPEG-2 Part 7 (ISO/IEC 13818-7, April) by Fraunhofer, Dolby, Sony, AT&T and Nokia; folded into MPEG-4 in 1999.
Igor Pavlov begins developing LZMA, later the core of 7-Zip; Fraunhofer/Thomson begin enforcing MP3 patent licensing (September), triggering the royalty-free codec movement.

JPEG 2000 adopts wavelet transforms as a multi-scale alternative to block DCT; Ferragina and Manzini introduce the FM-index, turning the BWT into a compressed searchable self-index; Matt Mahoney publishes 'Fast Text Compression with Neural Networks' (AAAI FLAIRS); Chris Montgomery's Xiph.Org freezes Ogg Vorbis 1.0 (May), a royalty-free MDCT codec.
JPEG 2000 (ISO 15444-1) finalized with wavelet coding; technically superior but adoption lagged due to memory/compute cost and patent uncertainty.
Josh Coalson releases FLAC 1.0 under a BSD license; Apple ships the iPod, accelerating MP3/AAC adoption.
Mahoney releases PAQ1, launching the PAQ context-mixing family; Dmitry Shkarin's PPMII/PPMd sets the PPM ratio standard.
H.264/AVC finalized (May) by the Joint Video Team of ITU-T VCEG and ISO/IEC MPEG; ~2x MPEG-2 efficiency, the long-running workhorse codec.
Marcus Hutter founds the Hutter Prize on enwik8, framing compression as a proxy for intelligence; Mahoney's paq8f sets the baseline and Alexander Rhatushnyak wins with paq8hp5.

Jarek Duda proposes Asymmetric Numeral Systems (ANS); core IBM arithmetic-coding patents (e.g. US 4,905,297) lapse, but the ecosystem has long since standardized on Huffman; Legg and Hutter publish a universal mathematical measure of machine intelligence.
Jarek Duda publishes the ANS paper (arXiv:0902.0271), refined through ~2013; Bowtie (Langmead et al.) and BWA (Li and Durbin) use FM-indexes for fast genomic read alignment.
Mahoney develops ZPAQ, a self-describing archival context-mixing format.
Google acquires On2 (Feb, ~$124.6M) and releases royalty-free VP8 (May) in the WebM container; Google announces WebP, based on VP8's intra coding.

Yann Collet releases LZ4 and Google releases Snappy (speed-first LZ codecs); CRAM reference-based genomic format introduced (Fritz et al.); product quantization for vector search introduced (Jegou, Douze, Schmid).
Opus standardized as IETF RFC 6716 (September), combining SILK and CELT for royalty-free low-latency audio.
H.265/HEVC finalized (~50% over H.264) but hit by patent-pool fragmentation; Google's VP9 released (June); Apache Parquet columnar format released, based on Google's Dremel design.
zfp error-bounded floating-point array compressor introduced by Peter Lindstrom (LLNL).

Facebook's Zstandard and Apple's LZFSE ship using ANS for entropy coding; Google open-sources Brotli (Alakuijala and Szabadka; RFC 7932 in 2016); HEIF container standardized by MPEG (HEIC pairs it with HEVC/H.265); Alliance for Open Media (AOMedia) formed (Sept); Gorilla time-series compression (Pelkonen et al., VLDB); Deep Compression pruning (Han, Mao, Dally) and knowledge distillation (Hinton, Vinyals, Dean).
Yann Collet releases Zstandard v1.0 at Facebook (Aug 31; later RFC 8878); SZ prediction-based error-bounded lossy compressor introduced (Di and Cappello, Argonne).

Johannes Balle, Valero Laparra, and Eero Simoncelli introduce end-to-end optimized image compression and the quantization-as-noise trick (ICLR 2017, with a 2016 GDN precursor), launching learned nonlinear transform coding via rate-distortion-optimized autoencoders (Balle and Theis, independently).

zstd enters the Linux kernel (4.14, November) for btrfs/squashfs/modules; Rhatushnyak's phda9 wins the enwik8-era Hutter Prize (15,284,944 bytes, Nov 4); Apple makes HEIC the default iPhone photo format; the last relevant MP3 US patents expire (~April) and Fraunhofer terminates its MP3 licensing program; Google's PIK and the JPEG XL effort adopt ANS as their entropy coder.

AV1 released by AOMedia (royalty-free, ~30% better than HEVC; YouTube begins deployment); Balle, Minnen et al. publish the scale hyperprior (ICLR), and Minnen, Balle, Toderici add an autoregressive context model (NeurIPS), the first learned codec to beat BPG on PSNR and MS-SSIM; after USPTO rejection and EFF advocacy, Google abandons its broad ANS patent application; Gagie, Navarro and Prezza present the r-index (SODA), an O(r)-space BWT index for highly repetitive texts.

AVIF specification 1.0 released (Feb 19), the royalty-free AV1-based image format from AOMedia; Google's PIK adopts ANS; Blau and Michaeli formalize the rate-distortion-perception tradeoff; Lu et al. present DVC (CVPR), the first fully end-to-end neural video codec; Fabrice Bellard releases the first nncp neural lossless compressor (LSTM-based).

H.266/VVC finalized (6 July), ~50% over HEVC; Netflix begins AV1 streaming; the Hutter Prize switches to the 1 GB enwik9 and expands the purse to 500,000 euro; HiFiC (Mentzer et al., NeurIPS) uses a GAN loss for high-fidelity generative image compression preferred over MSE codecs at half the bitrate; AVIF gains browser support starting with Chrome 85.

nncp v2 switches to a Transformer architecture, topping the Large Text Compression Benchmark; COIN (Dupont et al., ICLR workshop) compresses images by overfitting and storing a small network's weights; Microsoft's DCVC conditional-coding video line begins; LoRA low-rank adaptation introduced (Hu et al.); Firefox 93 adds AVIF support.
JPEG XL (ISO/IEC 18181) finalized, merging Google's PIK and Cloudinary's FUIF; royalty-free, ANS-based, with lossless JPEG transcoding.

Microsoft obtains a US patent on an rANS modification despite Duda's prior art, reviving patent-thicket concerns; GPTQ one-shot LLM weight quantization (Frantar et al.); CRAM 3.1 published; Safari 16.1 adds AVIF support (reaching >95% of users).

Delétang, Ruoss et al. (Google DeepMind) publish 'Language Modeling Is Compression' (arXiv:2309.10668), showing a 70B Chinchilla LLM + arithmetic coding compresses LibriSpeech to 16.4% (vs FLAC's 30.3%) and ImageNet patches to 43.4% (vs PNG's 58.5%), empirically uniting prediction and compression; Saurabh Kumar's fast-cmix wins the Hutter Prize; Bellard releases ts_zip; Google removes JPEG XL from Chrome 110 citing weak ecosystem interest and memory-safety concerns, sparking community backlash.

Kaido Orav's fx-cmix (Feb), then fx2-cmix with Byron Knoll (submitted Aug 11, accepted Oct 8) set the current enwik9 Hutter Prize record of 110,793,128 bytes (7,950 euro); 'Language Modeling Is Compression' presented at ICLR; Huang, Zhang, Shan, He publish 'Compression Represents Intelligence Linearly' (r about -0.95); Opus 1.5 (March) adds ML tools LACE/NoLACE and DRED; AWQ wins MLSys Best Paper; DeepSeek-V2 (May) and V3 (Dec) ship Multi-head Latent Attention; CUSZ-i GPU compressor at SC24; JARVIS3 genomic encoder; DCVC-DC/FM reportedly surpass VVC/H.266; JPEG AI's first edition published (October).

JPEG AI approved as an ISO/IEC/ITU International Standard (Jan); zstd v1.5.7 (February) adds hooks for Intel QAT hardware-accelerated match finding; one-step diffusion codecs StableCodec (ICCV) and OneDC (NeurIPS) reach ultra-low bitrates below 0.01 bpp; Trifecta Tech Foundation ships libbzip2-rs / bzip2 crate 0.6.0 (June), a 100% Rust reimplementation, and a dynamic r-index appears on arXiv (April); Access Advance acquires Via-LA's HEVC/VVC pools (Dec).

Chrome 145 (Feb 10/11, 2026) re-adds JPEG XL via a memory-safe Rust decoder (jxl-rs), with default-on enablement expected across Chromium/Edge in H2 2026; sub-2-bit vector quantization (VPTQ, QTIP), KV-cache compression (PolarQuant, AISTATS 2026), and LLM-assisted genomic compressors (AgentGC) push the frontier; AOMedia year-end-2025 AV2 launch (~30% over AV1) rolls out; compression rate is adopted as a robustness/quality metric for evaluating LLMs, with consensus shifting to viewing compression as a necessary signature of capable models rather than a complete definition of intelligence; Opus remains the reference real-time/VoIP/WebRTC codec.

corrections: Deduplicated and merged numerous repeated milestones that appeared across draft sections: Shannon 1948 (3 copies), Shannon 1959 rate-distortion (2 copies), Solomonoff algorithmic probability (3 copies), Kolmogorov/Chaitin complexity (2 copies), bzip2 1996 (2 copies), Princen/Bradley MDCT 1986-1987 (2 copies), JPEG 1992 (2 copies), Rissanen arithmetic coding 1976 (2 copies), Hutter Prize 2006 (2 copies), ANS/Duda 2007-2009 (2 copies), fx2-cmix Hutter record (3 copies), Blau-Michaeli RDP tradeoff (2 copies), and the DeepMind 'Language Modeling Is Compression' paper (4 copies).

Standardized the dating of DeepMind's 'Language Modeling Is Compression': arXiv preprint is September 2023 (arXiv:2309.10668) and the paper was presented at ICLR 2024 — both facts retained rather than treating the two draft entries (2023 vs 2024) as conflicting/separate events. All quantitative claims verified (LibriSpeech 16.4% vs FLAC 30.3%, ImageNet patches 43.4% vs PNG 58.5%).

Reconciled the MP3 standardization date: one draft entry said '1993' and another said '1991-1993'. Verified that the correct framing is the multi-year ISO process (committee draft 1991, finalized 1992, published 1993 as ISO/IEC 11172-3); used '1991-1993' and removed the standalone '1993 standardized' claim.

Verified the LZW timeline: Welch's paper is 1984 (IEEE Computer, June) and the LZW patent US 4,558,302 issued December 1985 — split into separate 1984 and 1985 entries to avoid conflating publication with patent issuance.

Confirmed (no change needed) several easily-mistaken facts: Shannon 1959 rate-distortion paper title and venue; Manber-Myers suffix arrays 1990 (SODA); Burrows-Wheeler DEC SRC Research Report 124 (1994); Witten-Neal-Cleary arithmetic coding 1987 (CACM); Ahmed-Natarajan-Rao DCT 1974 (IEEE Trans. Computers); H.266/VVC finalized 6 July 2020; AVIF 1.0 spec 19 Feb 2019; zstd Content-Encoding in Chrome 123 (March 2024) and Firefox 126 (May 2024); phda9 Hutter Prize record 15,284,944 bytes (Nov 2017); fx2-cmix enwik9 record 110,793,128 bytes; Chrome 145 JPEG XL re-add via jxl-rs (Feb 2026).

Reorganized all entries into a single chronologically sorted master timeline, placing multi-year-range entries (e.g. 1991-1993, 2016-2017) at the start year of their range and folding same-year events from different draft sections together.
